system_definition: The distributable Knowledge Plane is a permissively-licensed, single-language Python 3.12+/uv-workspace product (packages kp-core, kp-ingest, kp-index, kp-zotero, kp-mcp, kp-librarian — no kp-sync; mirror/feed/git plumbing folds into kp-core/kp-mcp) that turns any markdown+YAML git directory (Obsidian recommended viewer, never a dependency) plus an optional Zotero account into one addressable substrate under the sacred canonical/derived split: the vault and Zotero are canonical; the single embedded index.db (sqlite-vec + SQLite FTS5 + relational claim/link edge tables, WAL, multi-process-safe — Kuzu and LanceDB permanently dropped) is derived, disposable, and never migrated (blue/green epoch rebuilds keyed only on embedding-model+dims/chunker/normalization versions). It exposes exactly four published contracts (kp-note/v1: origin enum zotero|web|feed|manual, bare-hex body checksum as change-token-never-identity, no status field anywhere — lifecycle lives index-side keyed on producer-namespaced note_id curio:<uuid7>|zotero:<itemKey>|kp:<uuid7>; kp-config/v1; proposals/v1 branch-and-validator convention; the MCP tool surface) and one combined stateless FastMCP entrypoint (stdio default, Streamable HTTP optional; namespaced tool groups: retrieval search_hybrid/find_contradicting/unlinked_neighbors/similar_to_text/index_status, vault reads + propose_note/propose_patch as the ONLY write verbs, Zotero read-only two-channel: web-API metadata incl. /deleted tombstones + official /fulltext primary with a ~30-line WebDAV .prop/.zip on-demand fallback). Native Curio integration is by adapter, never template: the KP validates vanilla frontmatter-v1/events-v1 against vendored pinned schemas, treats .curio/manifest.json (read from merge-base) as the write-ownership oracle, enriches via companion notes only, and tails the events file sink with (filename,line) cursors. The librarian is deterministic-first: inbox drain and a baseline digest (embedding match vs now.md threads + recency decay) require zero LLM; Claude Code is the recommended optional enhancer harness, and every agent write lands on proposals/* through a local-first kp propose/review/apply validator (the same importable module runs as optional forge CI hardening — the safety model works with no git remote at all). Pluggable seams — OpenAI-compatible LLM/embeddings with an in-process pinned CPU ONNX embedder default, store driver interface (embedded-only at v1, Postgres a post-v1 profile), notifier (none|stdout|webhook|push-notifier), harness, git remote, Zotero metadata/attachment backends — mean llm-gateway, llm-observability, managed-postgres, git-forge, push-notifier, and the G11/pve topology are exclusively Alex's instance-repo configuration, absent from product code by a CI grep litmus. The repo GOES PUBLIC at Phase 6 (OSS launch), gated on ≥8 consecutive weeks of Alex's instance surviving on the same tagged code paths, the name/trademark and license decisions executed, gitleaks + license-audit green over the (public-safe-by-construction, fresh-scaffold) history, and a measured CI-passing quickstart — realistically Q1–Q2 2027 given interleave with Curio's 28-week plan.

phases.name: Phase 0 — Decisions, Curio schema review, repo split, CT 206
phases.goal: Lock the irreversible choices, file the only genuinely time-critical deliverable (the Curio schema review, months before Curio Phase 3 freezes), execute the three-repo split, land RFC-0019 + the ADR-0029 amendment, and unblock the next infra step (CT 206).
deliverables: Curio schema review filed as Curio-repo issues + a KP docs page (all items: negation events or documented monotone semantics; event_id ULID; tags in event article payload; ts format/ordering + cursor opacity + rotation/retention spec — these three declared BLOCKING outcomes for any KP dual-sink work; stable schema $id URLs/registry (W3); W1 per-feed private-network allowlist incl. explicit localhost semantics; managed-region marker reserved as ONE line in frontmatter-v1.md (W2); digest-feed convention documented (W6); manifest write-ordering + generation counter + merge-friendly manifest format; re-promotion must refuse on on-disk-checksum ≠ manifest-checksum drift)

RFC-0019 draft (companion RFC superseding stale RFC-0018 sections in one change set) + narrow ADR-0029 amendment (retrieval-follows-index) + short RFC-0016 note (community-canonical GitHub repo, Mode A reference mirror)

Fresh public-safe product repo scaffold on GitHub (private): Python uv workspace, 6 packages, justfile, hooks, .claude/ layer, kp.example.toml, zero LAN IPs/house-stack references (CI grep litmus from commit one); alex/knowledge-plane repurposed as the private git-forge instance repo; new private canonical vault repo created (Alex's manual git-forge action); now.md moves to the vault

kp-note/v1 + kp-config/v1 drafts (origin enum, bare-hex checksum, no status, producer-namespaced note_id, link policy: markdown relative links canonical, wikilinks accepted); Curio draft schemas vendored as sha-pinned fixtures labeled draft
Zotero official fulltext API verification (curl one-liner against /items/<key>/fulltext for a WebDAV-stored item) — decides the .prop/.zip shim demotion before any shim code exists

CT 206 created (Alex approves the staged pct create after re-confirming router-fw-cutover status and gw/DNS params): nginx WebDAV (dav_ext + basic-auth, htpasswd escrowed to secrets-manager), scoped to also absorb the vault mirror + kp-vault MCP later — no CT 207 ever
acceptance: Schema-review issues exist in the Curio repo cross-linked from KP docs, before Curio Phase 3 starts
grep -rE '192\.168\.68|llm-gateway|llm-observability|managed-postgres|git-forge|push-notifier|obsidian' over product-repo code/config returns nothing (docs may name Obsidian as recommended viewer)
Zotero desktop on the Mac syncs attachments to CT 206 WebDAV successfully; zotero.org metadata sync unchanged
/fulltext verification result recorded with an explicit go/no-go on the shim's role
RFC-0019 accepted; ADR-0029 amendment recorded; three repos exist with vault/ and now.md gone from the product repo
phases.size: 1-2 weeks calendar (2-4 days focused agent work + Alex decision latency); runs immediately, parallel with Curio Phase 0
phases.oss_state: Nothing runnable. A hypothetical stranger could read draft contracts and the architecture docs in a public-safe (but still private) repo; the history is publishable by construction from commit one.

phases.name: Phase 1 — Spine: vault, mirror, kp-vault MCP, local disposal
phases.goal: Canonical vault live and agent-addressable: reads via MCP from any client, writes only via proposals, with the full safety model working forge-free (the OSS default) and forge-hardened (Alex's instance).
deliverables: kp-core: kp.toml loader/validator (KP_* env + _FILE overrides), frontmatter parse per kp-note/v1, atomic tmp+rename writes, dual hashing (contract body-checksum as opaque change token + internal full-file hash for reindex), kp:<uuid7> identity minting, .curio/manifest.json reader

Vault repo populated (Alex migrates his GitHub Obsidian vault, his timeline): inbox/ (with curio/ subdir reserved), literature/, notes/, digests/, now.md; git-forge remote with branch protection (main protected; librarian token pushes proposals/* only)

Vault mirror on CT 206: bare repo + worktree, `git fetch --prune && git reset --hard origin/main` (never pull), push-webhook + cron fallback, read-only deploy key; non-fast-forward detection triggers automatic full sweep downstream

kp-vault MCP tool group (list/read/search/grep/open_proposals + propose_note/propose_patch as the only write verbs; server refuses manifest-listed paths and any .curio/** diff): stdio for OSS, Streamable HTTP behind Caddy at kp.example.internal for Alex; CT 206 holds TWO credentials — read-only mirror deploy key + a separate proposals-only push token (mirror-doctrine sentence amended accordingly)

Local disposal flow as the primary safety model: kp propose / kp review / kp apply running ONE importable validator module (per-schema frontmatter validation, path allowlist, merge-base manifest-ownership check, duplicate/absent-identity rejection across main + open proposals, link integrity, conflict-marker grep, cross-branch path-collision check, merge-queue re-validation on current main); the same module deployed as the vault repo's git-forge Actions gate (optional hardening tier)
Vault main-push report-only CI (conflict markers, schema, manifest-entry-resolves) + the Mac sync ritual written into the instance runbook (no autostash-rebase, explicit commit points)
acceptance: Claude Code on the Mac lists/reads/searches the vault through kp-vault MCP over HTTPS with a minted bearer token (kp token mint works)
A proposal branch round-trips end-to-end: propose → validator green → human merge → mirror updated within one webhook cycle; the identical flow works on a laptop with NO git remote via kp apply
Validator fixture suite passes: a branch editing the manifest AND a manifest-listed note is rejected; duplicate identity rejected; .curio/** diff rejected; conflict markers rejected
Force-push to vault main is survived: mirror hard-resets, full sweep scheduled, nothing freezes (tested)
phases.size: 2-3 weeks focused (3-5 weeks calendar interleaved with Curio Phase 1)
phases.oss_state: A stranger with repo access can point kp at any markdown directory and get the vault MCP surface + local propose/review/apply with zero forge, zero LLM, zero Zotero. Repo still private.

phases.name: Phase 2 — Ingestion: Zotero two-channel + web clips
phases.goal: The Zotero library flows into the vault as literature stubs with correct identity, deletion, and race handling; the RFC's nonexistent data path is replaced by the verified two-channel design.
deliverables: kp-zotero metadata channel: zotero.org Web API poller (Last-Modified-Version etiquette, backoff) into a disposable local cache; /deleted?since consumed in the same cycle with tombstone propagation (drop index rows/edges for zotero:<key>, raise an orphaned-note PROPOSAL — never auto-delete); metadata-backend SEAM defined in kp-config (web-api implemented now; local zotero.sqlite/localhost:23119 backend lands Phase 6)

Fulltext: official /items/<key>/fulltext API as primary (per Phase 0 verification); .prop/.zip WebDAV on-demand GET+unzip fallback (~30 lines: zip CRC verification before use, defer+retry on pair mismatch, orphan-.zip surfaced never auto-fixed, no cache-layout contract, no headline marketing); attachment-backend seam (webdav-local | zotero-cloud | local-dir)

Literature stubs via proposals keyed strictly on zotero:<itemKey>; citekey (BBT 'Citation Key:' Extra-field convention, deterministic fallback) is the filename/wikilink handle; a citekey change emits a RENAME proposal, never a new stub; validator rejects a duplicate zotero_key at a new path; metadata-before-attachment race handled (pending-retry state, fulltext-present index flag, attachment arrival emits index-invalidate)

Web-clip producer (kp:<uuid7> identity); OCR router with Docling (MIT) as the only default engine — PyMuPDF4LLM/Marker as user-installed subprocess-isolated extras, never in the dependency tree or images; low-confidence OCR to a review queue

Zotero annotations mirrored into a KP-owned COMPANION note (literature/<citekey>.annotations.md, regenerable, proposal-landed) — no managed regions, no writes into human-grown note bodies; mirrored spans excluded from chunking when duplicated in the parent body
kp-zotero MCP tool group on CT 206 (search_items/get_item/get_fulltext/get_annotations) — READ-ONLY; API key scoped read, in Keychain/secrets-manager
acceptance: A full library poll produces a valid stub proposal for every item; deleting an item in Zotero produces a tombstone + orphan proposal within one poll cycle (tested)
get_fulltext returns indexable text for a native-text PDF via the official API and PDF bytes via the WebDAV fallback for a scanned item
Re-pinning a BBT citekey yields a rename proposal with wikilink rewrite, zero duplicates (tested)
The Zotero API key has no write scope; no KP component can mutate Zotero (verified)
phases.oss_state: A stranger with a zotero.org account + read API key replicates Zotero→vault ingest end-to-end; self-hosted WebDAV is the documented advanced path, Zotero File Storage (zotero-cloud backend) the mainstream one. Repo still private.

phases.name: Phase 3 — Index + retrieval (numeric pve entry gate)
phases.goal: Hybrid retrieval over ONE embedded store co-located with its index on pve, operable via rebuild-not-migrate, entered only through a measured resource gate.
deliverables: BLOCKING ENTRY-GATE TASK (merges before any Phase 3 branch): live pve utilization (pvesh/free -h), Docling docs/hour, embedding chunks/sec on the chosen model, recorded with go/no-go thresholds; named fallback = reduced cadence/scope, not new hardware

kp-index: one index.db — sqlite-vec vectors + FTS5 BM25 + relational claim/link edge tables (recursive CTEs for one-hop expansion and supports/refutes), WAL, multi-process-safe; chunker with chunk-level content hashes (unchanged chunks skip re-embedding across rebuilds); embedder seam: in-process pinned CPU ONNX small model as DEFAULT + any OpenAI-compatible /v1/embeddings endpoint (Alex flips to Ollama-on-pve or llm-gateway by config)

Incremental reindex: commit-driven (internal vault.updated event — not a published contract) via name-status diff + full-file-hash comparison so frontmatter-only edits reindex metadata rows without re-embedding; non-FF ranges auto-schedule a full sweep; periodic sweep compares full-file hashes; file-watcher mode for single-box installs (semantic difference vs mirror-HEAD documented); indexer skips-and-alerts on conflict-markered files

Blue/green epochs: rebuild into a new index.db stamped with the new epoch, completeness check (note count + checksum coverage vs mirror), atomic flip, old epoch dropped after; epoch = f(embedding model+dims, chunker version, normalization version) ONLY — kp_version recorded but never invalidating; OCR cache keyed on attachment hash survives all epochs

kp-retrieval tool group (search_hybrid with weighted RRF + one-hop typed edge expansion and per-leg score breakdown, find_contradicting, unlinked_neighbors, similar_to_text, index_status incl. mirror-HEAD-vs-remote lag) served from the ONE combined kp-mcp entrypoint co-located with the index on pve — index query API is an in-process Python interface, not a network contract; citations = path+heading+checksum in MCP payloads (point-in-time), wikilink-by-basename + note_id in any canonical artifact

MCP DX: kp mcp install --client claude-code|claude-desktop|cursor|--print; kp token mint|list|revoke; FastMCP pinned at one protocol version; secure-by-default 127.0.0.1 binds; ADR-0029 amendment executed in the instance deployment (retrieval on pve; CT 206 keeps kp-vault + mirror + kp-zotero; zero cross-host data paths)
acceptance: Entry-gate numbers recorded and thresholds met (or scope formally reduced per the named fallback) before any index code merges
search_hybrid returns fused, cited results from Mac Claude Code against Alex's real corpus; find_contradicting answers a supports/refutes graph query

Frontmatter-only tag edit updates the index metadata row without re-embedding (test); embedding-model swap completes blue/green with zero retrieval downtime (test); kill -9 mid-rebuild leaves the serving epoch intact (test)
The full single-box demo path (ingest→index→search_hybrid) runs on a clean machine with NO Ollama, NO Postgres, NO forge — in-process embedder only
phases.size: 3-4 weeks focused (4-6 weeks calendar); Phases 1-3 together are the verdicts' ~6-8-week personal-instance spine plus the mandated correctness machinery
phases.oss_state: A stranger replicates the complete single-box core: clone → uv sync → kp init → ingest → index → search from any MCP client, zero external services. Repo still private — this is the future quickstart, not yet published.

phases.name: Phase 4 — Maintenance loop + deterministic baseline digest
phases.goal: The librarian drains the inbox and emits a useful digest with zero LLM required; Claude Code rides on top as Alex's optional enhancer. This completes KP v1 (the personal-instance spine).
deliverables: Deterministic CLI jobs the agent supervises but never improvises: inbox drain → OCR route → embed → dedupe via similar_to_text threshold → stub/companion proposals on one dated proposals/maintenance-* branch; librarian consults open_proposals + the index before proposing (no duplicate stubs)

Baseline digest v0 (pure code, no LLM): new-since-last-digest candidates ranked by embedding similarity to now.md thread embeddings + recency decay → digests/YYYY-MM-DD.md via proposals/digest-*; digests/ is CREATE-ONLY in the validator; re-runs idempotent by date; auto-merge does not exist — a human merges every branch; interest model = design doc only

Harness: Claude Code on the EXISTING claude-agent VM with its own RFC-0013 NFS workspace (no fourth agent guest), cron-scheduled, llm-gateway virtual key 'kp-librarian' with budget + llm-observability project via gateway callback (all instance config, zero product code); CLAUDE.md standing orders (proposals-only, one-digest-or-silence, contradiction-first, never touch manifest-listed paths); harness=none is a first-class supported mode; openai-loop runner demoted to contrib/

Notifier plugin (none|stdout|webhook|push-notifier) — Alex enables the ADR-0028-compliant LAN-pull push-notifier one-liner; always-on local JSONL run ledger (kp librarian runs) feeding the digest footer
proposals/v1 contract finalized and frozen with the other three (kp-note/v1, kp-config/v1, MCP tool surface) — per-contract CHANGELOGs; everything else stays internal
acceptance: A cron run drains a seeded inbox end-to-end producing validator-green proposals; an immediate re-run produces zero duplicates (open_proposals + idempotency tests)
The digest generated with harness=none is structurally valid and cites real notes; enabling Claude Code changes prose quality only, not artifact shape (diff test)
The librarian credential cannot push to vault main (forge protection verified) and the validator rejects the full attack-fixture suite
Alex's instance enters continuous operation — the ≥8-week survival clock for Phase 6 starts here
phases.oss_state: A stranger replicates the entire loop — ingest, index, retrieval, maintenance, digest — with no Claude subscription and no forge; Claude Code documented as the recommended enhancer. Repo still private, feature-complete for v1.

phases.name: Phase 5 — Curio native integration (calendar-gated on Curio Phase 3)
phases.goal: Vanilla frontmatter-v1/events-v1 consumption flips from draft fixtures to released artifacts; the reading surface plugs in as pure configuration on both sides. BLOCKS ON Curio Phase 3 shipping contracts (~11-14.5 weeks into Curio's 28-week plan, ~Q4 2026); FEEDS Curio Phase 3 before that: the schema review (Phase 0) and the KP-shaped inbox + fixtures (Phases 1-2) are what Curio's generic-consumer acceptance test demos against.
deliverables: kp-ingest curio adapter GA (feature-flagged preview until here): validates vanilla frontmatter-v1 against pinned released schemas (additive minors tolerated, major mismatch → index-side quarantine FLAG + alert, never a file move), maps fields, keys on curio:<uuid7>, lifecycle index-side

Events consumption: file-sink tail with (filename,line) rotation-aware cursors in the XDG state dir; sha256(raw line) LRU dedupe until Curio's event_id lands; .curio/events/ gitignored EVERYWHERE including Alex's vault (manifest.json stays committed); events are hints — promoted-before-push race handled via pending-expectations with the mirror diff as sole ingest truth; kp events replay CLI (Mac-side re-feed) ships with it

kp vault autocommit (launchd/systemd user unit) shipped as a named product component WITH quiesce logic: manifest mtime stable ≥N s, every manifest entry resolves, no .tmp files — the torn note+manifest pair generator is designed out; Alex's Curio destination = vault inbox/curio/

Ownership enforcement live end-to-end: validator reads manifest from merge-base, main-push CI checks manifest consistency, checksum verification discriminates by identity namespace (curio:* opaque, kp:*/zotero:* verified), Mac-side hooks exempt manifest-listed paths
Companion-note enrichment (notes/re/<slug>.md wikilinking Curio notes) via proposals — the shipped mechanism; managed regions remain a paper reservation

Cross-repo contract CI flips from allow-fail (draft fixtures) to hard-fail (Curio's released schema artifacts); joint run of Curio's wipe-and-reinstall reconcile and generic-consumer scripts against the real KP vault
acceptance: Curio promote on the Mac → autocommit → mirror → indexed, with the measured latency documented as the honest contract (no 'within minutes' claim unverified)
Curio re-promotion of an article the KP has enriched clobbers nothing (lifecycle index-side + companion-note tests)
Curio wipe-and-reinstall against the KP vault reconciles with zero duplicate notes (Curio's own CI-gated criterion, run jointly)
Cursor survives file rotation; replayed event lines dedupe; read/star/promote events mutate nothing canonical
phases.size: 2-3 weeks focused; start date is Curio-Phase-3-gated (~Q4 2026) — KP Phases 3-4 and Curio Phases 1-2 fill the intervening calendar
phases.oss_state: A stranger with ANY frontmatter-v1-conforming producer plugs a reading surface into their KP by config alone; Curio named in docs only as 'preview' until its trademark clearance lands. Repo still private.

phases.name: Phase 6 — OSS hardening + public launch (gated on ≥8 weeks instance survival)
phases.goal: The repo goes public and the quickstart promise is machine-true. Entry gates: ≥8 consecutive weeks of Alex-instance operation on tagged code, name + license decisions executed, gitleaks + license-audit green.
deliverables: Docs: quickstart with ONE measured wall-clock number from a bare runner including prerequisites box; concepts/canonical-derived; integrations (curio, zotero, obsidian-as-viewer, generic-reader); operations (backup, rebuild, upgrade-by-rebuild); reference (config, CLI, MCP tools); tested-MCP-clients matrix replacing 'any client works'

examples/sample-vault + just demo; kp init (+ --claude vault starter: CLAUDE.md + /research /library /contradictions skills); examples/ RSS-to-kp-note script (no fetcher package — 'any frontmatter-v1 producer' is the contract answer); local Zotero metadata backend (zotero.sqlite snapshot / localhost:23119) lands here

CI: linux per-commit fast gate with mock embedder; weekly scheduled real end-to-end (pinned ONNX model cached) as a release blocker; gitleaks; license-audit gate proving copyleft OCR extras stay out of the dependency tree; macOS as a non-blocking smoke

Packaging: ONE compose profile set (core | ollama | zotero | librarian, CPU-only) + uvx/pip+systemd as the co-equal documented mode (what Alex actually runs — both modes have a real continuously-exercised user); installs via git tags + uvx-from-git; no PyPI/ghcr/Registry yet

Launch mechanics: LICENSE (per Alex's decision), GOVERNANCE.md, SECURITY.md, DCO; repo flipped public on GitHub; RFC-0016 Mode A reference pull-mirror into git-forge mirrors/; instance repo pins the product at the launch tag with the pin-bump PR ritual + a zero-forked-code representativeness check in instance CI
acceptance: A clean-machine quickstart run by someone-not-Alex completes ingest→index→search under the published measured number
CI litmus green: the demo references none of Alex's stack; Alex's instance runs the identical tag with config-only overrides (zero forked code, verified)
≥8 weeks of run-ledger evidence of continuous instance operation predates the public flip
gitleaks + license-audit green over full history; the weekly real end-to-end job has been green 4 consecutive weeks
phases.size: 3-4 weeks focused; the ≥8-week survival gate makes the calendar floor ~2 months after Phase 4 completes — public launch realistically Q1-Q2 2027
phases.oss_state: REPO PUBLIC. This is the publication moment: a stranger replicates the entire system unaided from the public quickstart, and the four contracts have stable public artifacts.

phases.name: Phase 7 — Post-launch menu (each item independently gated)
phases.goal: Depth features ship only when their preconditions exist; nothing here blocks or is assumed by Phases 0-6.
deliverables: Interest model v1 (event-boosted re-rank strictly inside now.md thread scope, inspectable state-dir artifact + digest-rendered report) — gate: months of corpus + events flowing + the negation-events schema-review outcome known

Atom digest feed + Curio subscription closing the digest→read/star/promote loop — gate: Curio W1 private-network allowlist shipped AND trademark cleared; feed is a static file, digest note stays primary
Curio webhook events receiver (bearer-auth, schema-validated, replay-idempotent) — gate: file-sink-tail latency proven insufficient in practice

Postgres/pgvector store driver profile (identical edge DDL) — gate: a real user needs cross-host or scale; pgvector one-liner + managed-postgres PG15→17 resolved first; compose.split stays a docs page
Managed-region in-note enrichment + Zotero annotation regions — gate: Curio ships region-scoped re-export honoring the reserved marker

Release train (PyPI, ghcr CPU multi-arch, MCP Registry publication, release.yml) — gate: external users exist; openai-loop harness promotion from contrib/ — gate: demonstrated demand + CI budget; Zotero write tools (staging-collection-enforced add_by_identifier) — gate: a review-then-move ritual designed; CPU reranker profile — gate: measured retrieval-quality need
acceptance: Each item merges with its gate evidence recorded in the PR body
No Phase 0-6 artifact ever depended on an item from this list (audited at Phase 6 launch)
phases.size: Ongoing; individually 1-2 focused weeks each, scheduled opportunistically against Curio Phases 4-6
phases.oss_state: Baseline unchanged; each landed item widens what a stranger gets (network deployment profile, in-reader digest loop, behavioral ranking) without ever narrowing the zero-infra core.

rfc_changes: MECHANISM: no nine in-place edits to the accepted RFC-0018. One companion RFC-0019 ('The Knowledge Plane as a distributable system — and data-path corrections') supersedes the stale RFC-0018 sections in a single change set; RFC-0018 gets a one-line amendment banner pointing at RFC-0019; ADR-0029 gets exactly one narrow amendment; RFC-0016 gets a short process note. All four land in Phase 0.

RFC-0018 §Design/Index, superseded: 'full-text/BM25 (free via the Obsidian REST plugin/Omnisearch)' — that implementation died with the live-desktop-app dependency ADR-0029 removed and violates Obsidian-never-a-dependency. The system owns FTS: SQLite FTS5 in the embedded default (Postgres tsvector only in the post-v1 profile), behind the store driver interface.

RFC-0018 §Design/Index graph store, superseded: the claim/link graph is relational edge tables inside the SAME index store (recursive CTEs for one-hop expansion and supports/refutes at personal scale). Kuzu and LanceDB are dropped permanently — Kuzu additionally lacks safe multi-process concurrent access, which the stdio-MCP + indexer-daemon architecture requires; sqlite WAL handles it.

RFC-0018 §Design/Access + §Placement table, superseded: 'a Zotero server reads zotero.sqlite directly' and 'Zotero MCP reads a WebDAV-synced local copy of the library' describe data paths that do not exist (WebDAV carries only attachments as unsupported .prop/.zip pairs; metadata rides zotero.org; the web API cannot serve WebDAV-stored files). Corrected to the verified two-channel design: Web API metadata (with /deleted?since tombstones) + the official /items/<key>/fulltext channel primary, WebDAV .prop/.zip demoted to a ~30-line on-demand fallback for scanned PDFs. CT 206 colocation is strengthened, not amended.

RFC-0018 §Design/Access, superseded: 'an Obsidian server (needs the live desktop app)' — the body text was never re-edited after ADR-0029's amendment; the vault MCP is the in-repo kp-vault filesystem surface over the git mirror, and the corollary is recorded: Obsidian is the recommended viewer, never a dependency.

RFC-0018 §Alternatives, retracted: 'build custom MCP servers from scratch — rejected; mature options exist... only retrieval-fusion is genuinely custom.' All three MCP tool groups are built in-repo as one combined kp-mcp entrypoint: the named candidates require the live app, read a sqlite file that never reaches the server host, or bundle a second embedding store (54yyyu's ChromaDB) violating single-derived-index discipline. They stay thin wrappers over pyzotero/FastMCP/git.

RFC-0018 §Placement + §Librarian, respecified under ADR-0028: 'push-notifier digest delivery' becomes — primary artifact is the digest note (digests/YYYY-MM-DD.md via a human-merged proposals/digest-* branch, create-only); notifier is pluggable (none|stdout|webhook|push-notifier LAN-pull); no cloud push path exists or may be added; the Atom feed Curio subscribes to is deferred post-v1 pending Curio W1.

RFC-0018 §Ingestion + §Rollout Phase 2, re-scoped: the KP-built RSS fetcher is removed from the product (an examples/ script + the 'any frontmatter-v1-conforming producer' contract is the OSS answer); Curio's frontmatter-v1 + events-v1 are the primary reading-surface path; Phase 2 ingestion = Zotero + web clips (transcripts later). The mandatory Curio schema review is recorded as a KP deliverable filed as consumer #1.

RFC-0018 §Design spine 'frontmatter schema locked', unfrozen as the deliberate migration the schema doc's own rule requires: kp-note/v1 — Curio-anchored (source=URL, saved/published, schema_version), origin enum (zotero|web|feed|manual), bare-hex sha256 body checksum as a change token NEVER an identity key, no status field in any profile (lifecycle is index-side for ALL notes, keyed on producer-namespaced note_id curio:<uuid7>|zotero:<itemKey>|kp:<uuid7>, minted at ingest), link policy declared (markdown relative links canonical, wikilinks accepted). One schema with optional fields — no five-profile system, no 'kp-frontmatter-v2'.

RFC-0018 §Open Questions LanceDB-vs-pgvector, resolved differently than the recorded lean: the embedded store (sqlite-vec + FTS5 + edge tables) is the default AND Alex's v1 instance choice — the cross-host argument that forced the pgvector lean dissolves because kp-retrieval co-locates in-process with its index on pve. Store choice is configuration behind a driver interface; Postgres/pgvector is a post-v1 profile. The unverified-managed-postgres-pgvector and PG15→17 risks leave the critical path.

RFC-0018 §Repo boundary + '@plane/*' naming, superseded: three-repo split — public product repo (fresh scaffold, GitHub-canonical, Python 3.12+/uv workspace, Node dropped), private instance repo (repurposed git-forge alex/knowledge-plane: real endpoints, compose/Ansible overlays, product pinned at release tags), private canonical vault repo (absorbs vault/ and now.md). The management plane installs the PRODUCT at the instance-recorded tag (pip/image pin, not a submodule) and templates config at apply time.

RFC-0018 §Design/Librarian, extended with the disposal side it omits: the local-first kp propose/review/apply validator (one importable module: schema, path allowlist, merge-base manifest ownership, identity uniqueness, link integrity) is the DEFAULT safety model requiring no forge; forge CI + branch protection + proposals-only credentials are optional hardening; loops are deterministic-first (maintenance drain and baseline digest need zero LLM; Claude Code is the recommended optional harness — no Anthropic subscription required for baseline value).

NEW RFC-0019 scope (the distributable-system RFC): the product/instance/vault split and estate wiring; licensing + naming + governance (DCO); exactly four published contracts (kp-note/v1, kp-config/v1, proposals/v1, MCP tool surface) with the versioning policy — everything else internal; the pluggable seams (OpenAI-compatible LLM/embeddings with in-process CPU ONNX default, store driver, notifier, harness, git remote, Zotero metadata/attachment backends); the derived-never-migrates operability story (blue/green epochs keyed on model/chunker/normalization only, rebuild is the upgrade); the two phase gates (numeric pve entry gate before Phase 3; ≥8-week instance-survival gate before publication); the Curio consumer-#1 relationship (vendored pinned schemas, schema review, ownership rules, adapter-never-template); the instance-representativeness litmus (CI grep + zero-forked-code check).

ADR-0029, ONE narrow amendment: the retrieval MCP follows its index — kp-retrieval runs in-process with kp-index on pve (an always-on G11 adapter fronting an index on the reboot-freely host bought nothing and cost a forever-maintained network contract); 'MCP on G11' continues to hold for kp-vault + the git mirror + kp-zotero, ALL folded into CT 206 (no CT 207); the librarian rides the existing claude-agent VM with its own RFC-0013 workspace (no fourth agent guest — softening the ADR's 'fourth agent guest' consequence); the pve measurement precondition is hardened from prose to a numeric blocking entry-gate task. Every other ADR-0029 placement stands and becomes instance-side configuration under the split — which validates the decision.

RFC-0016, short process note: a community-canonical (GitHub-first) repo is a new case neither mode covers — the product repo is GitHub-canonical with a Mode A reference pull-mirror into git-forge mirrors/ for estate visibility; development happens Mac-side against GitHub; nobody commits git-forge-side to a repo whose truth lives on GitHub.
alex_decisions: NAME (blocks publication, not development): 'knowledge-plane' is unbrandable and collides with the Clark-et-al networking term — pick the product name and run Curio's clearance ritual (PyPI + GitHub org + domain + trademark). Needed by Phase 6; placeholder <kp> until then.

LICENSE: recommend MIT OR Apache-2.0 dual (symmetry with Curio; AGPL rejected on Curio's own reasoning — open contracts, not copyleft, are the lock-in defense); DCO not CLA. Corollary he must accept: Docling-only default OCR, copyleft engines as subprocess-isolated user-installed extras with a CI license-audit gate.

REPO TOPOLOGY (Phase 0, semi-irreversible): fresh public-safe product scaffold on GitHub (private until Phase 6 launch, GitHub-canonical thereafter with an RFC-0016 Mode A mirror into git-forge mirrors/); repurpose git-forge alex/knowledge-plane as the private instance repo; create the private canonical vault repo (his manual git-forge UI action — the IaC token cannot); now.md moves into the vault.

LANGUAGE (irreversible once code lands): commit to Python 3.12+/uv workspace and drop Node — reverses the scaffold's package.json and RFC-0018's '@plane/*' prose. Curio owns Rust; the seam is file contracts.

CT 206 APPROVAL — the next infra step, pending across sessions: approve the staged pct create AFTER re-confirming router-fw cutover status and network params (gw 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.69), and approve its widened scope (WebDAV + kp-zotero + vault mirror + kp-vault MCP on one CT; CT 207 is cancelled). Per house rule this belongs in the owner: alex Home queue, not chat.

ADR-0029 AMENDMENT SIGN-OFF: retrieval MCP moves to pve with its index; librarian reuses the claude-agent VM (no fourth agent guest); the pve numeric entry gate becomes blocking. This is the one accepted-decision change — everything else in ADR-0029 stands.

SCHEMA UNFREEZE: approve superseding the scaffold's 'locked' frontmatter schema with kp-note/v1 (origin enum, bare-hex checksum, NO status field, producer-namespaced identity) — a deliberate migration under his own schema doc's rule, executed before any consumer code exists.

CURIO SCHEMA REVIEW FILING: authorize filing the full review (negation events, event_id ULID, tags-in-payload, ts/cursor/rotation spec, schema $ids, W1 LAN+localhost allowlist, managed-region marker reservation, digest-feed convention, manifest write-ordering + mergeable format, re-promotion drift refusal) as Curio issues NOW — it expands Curio's scope and he owns both plans; the KP treats event_id/ts/rotation as blocking outcomes for dual-sink work.

CANONICAL MIGRATIONS (his manual work, his timeline — gates Phase 1/2 completion): GitHub Obsidian vault → the new canonical vault repo; Zotero attachments → CT 206 WebDAV; mint a READ-ONLY Zotero API key into Keychain with secrets-manager escrow per the secrets registry.

EVENTS PRIVACY REVERSAL: accept .curio/events/ gitignored even in his private vault (XDG state dir is the product default; .curio/manifest.json stays committed — the reconcile story requires it). This overrides the earlier in-vault-committed lean; unbounded behavioral JSONL in git is bad hygiene his own designs named.

VAULT-REPO CREDENTIAL MODEL: approve two credentials on CT 206 (read-only mirror deploy key + a separate proposals-only push token for propose_*) and git-forge branch protection on the vault repo (main protected, librarian token restricted to proposals/*; verify git-forge can express this — degraded mode documented honestly if not).

PUBLICATION GATE: agree the repo flips public only after ≥8 consecutive weeks of instance survival on tagged code + name/license executed + gitleaks/license-audit green — realistically Q1-Q2 2027; 'distributable' before then means seams + public-safe repo hygiene, not launch.
REVIEW OVERHEAD: confirm he accepts human-merges-everything at v1 (no digest auto-merge, no auto-merged stubs) — the validator earns autonomy with months of history first.
cut_list: Postgres/pgvector store driver at v1 — post-v1 profile (Phase 7); Alex runs the embedded store on pve, deleting the managed-postgres coupling, the pgvector verification, and the PG15→17 one-way migration from the critical path, and making his instance exercise the exact OSS default code path.
Kuzu and LanceDB — dropped permanently (relational edge tables in the one store; Kuzu is additionally multi-process-unsafe under the stdio-MCP + indexer architecture).
CT 207 and the fourth agent VM on pve — mirror + kp-vault MCP fold into CT 206; the librarian rides the existing claude-agent VM with its own RFC-0013 workspace.
The versioned HTTP index query API — an in-process Python interface only; kp-retrieval co-locates with its index (zero cross-host data paths).

Published-contract sprawl: kp-events bus, interest-model artifact schema, attachment-cache layout, and the index API all become internal; digest-feed becomes a docs paragraph (Atom, stable GUIDs); managed-region becomes a one-line marker reservation inside the Curio schema review; ranking-hints.v1 stays name-reserved only. Exactly four contracts are published.
The kp-note five-profile schema system — one kp-note/v1 with optional fields; Curio-owned notes validate directly against the vendored frontmatter-v1.
'kp-frontmatter-v2' wholesale (prefixed checksum, status-on-KP-notes, path+checksum identity) and checksum-as-identity anywhere — identity is never derived from content or path.

The discovery loop / interest model implementation (thread+claim vectors, source-type priors, decay half-lives, artifact schema, report renderer) — design doc only until the maintenance loop has months of corpus; digest v0 is the deterministic embedding-match-vs-now.md query.
The OpenAI-compatible neutral librarian runner as a supported v1 surface — contrib/example; harness neutrality is guaranteed by the proposals/v1 contract and the deterministic-baseline loops instead.
The Atom digest feed + Curio subscription loop at v1 — double-blocked on Curio W1 and trademark; digest note + optional push-notifier LAN ping covers Alex (Phase 7 when gates clear).
The Curio webhook events receiver at v1 — file-sink tail with (filename,line) cursors; the receiver is Phase 7, gated on proven latency need; byte-offset cursors cut outright.

In-note managed regions at v1, including the Zotero annotation region mirror — companion notes are the shipped mechanism on both the Curio and Zotero sides; the marker stays a paper reservation with Curio.
Zotero write tools (add_by_identifier / add_item) — Zotero MCP is read-only at v1; writes wait for an enforced staging-collection + review ritual (Phase 7).
Digest auto-merge machinery in any form — a human merges every proposals/* branch at v1; digests/ is create-only in the validator regardless.

The minimal RSS fallback fetcher package — an examples/ script emitting kp-note/v1 plus the 'any frontmatter-v1-conforming producer' contract is the OSS answer (feed parsing is a maintenance tar-pit duplicating Curio).

The .prop/.zip WebDAV extraction shim as a headline feature with cache layout + published contract — demoted (after the Phase 0 /fulltext API verification) to a ~30-line CRC-verified on-demand fallback for scanned PDFs; the 'nobody serves this niche' differentiator marketing is cut.
MCP Registry publication, PyPI packages, ghcr multi-arch images, and release.yml — Phase 7, gated on external users existing; until then git tags + uvx-from-git + compose-from-source.

Per-commit real-Ollama demo-smoke and blocking macOS CI — mock-embedder fast gate on linux per commit; the real end-to-end (in-process pinned ONNX model, cached) runs weekly as a release blocker; macOS is a non-blocking smoke until launch.
Dual MCP protocol-version support — pin FastMCP at one protocol version; transport isolation in kp-mcp makes any spec delta one package's problem.
The CPU reranker (bge-reranker) profile — cut entirely at v1 (Phase 7 gated on measured retrieval-quality need).
compose.split.yml — no real consumer (Alex's split is Ansible-provisioned systemd); a docs page describes split deployment; one compose profile taxonomy survives (core | ollama | zotero | librarian).
The seventh 'kp-sync' package — mirror/feed/webhook/git plumbing folds into kp-core and kp-mcp; six packages total.
Direct vault append/patch MCP tools — propose_note/propose_patch are the only write verbs that exist anywhere.
The naive vault autocommit watcher — ships only in Phase 5 WITH quiesce logic (manifest-stable + entries-resolve + no-.tmp); a quiesce-free watcher is the torn-pair generator and never ships.
Nine separate in-place RFC-0018 amendment edits — one companion RFC-0019 supersedes the stale sections in a single change set.
The 'file sink retained for replay/backfill' claim in its v1 form — either the kp events replay CLI ships (it does, Phase 5) or the claim doesn't exist; no unbacked backfill promises.
